modals.worsenedTitle: All countries — inequality change (first → latest reading)
modals.improvedTitle: All countries — inequality improvement (first → latest reading)
modals.changesTitle: All countries — full Gini snapshot table

sections.continentalAvg: Continental avg Gini — year-by-year (survey years only)
sections.continentalAvgDesc: Each point = avg of all country readings in that survey year. Data is sparse — not every country reported every year.
sections.dataCoverage: Data coverage — number of survey readings per country (2000–2020)

notableNotes.consistentlyLow: Consistently low — most equal in Africa

insights.southernAfricaDesc: South Africa (63.0), Namibia (59.1), Zambia (55.8), and Botswana (54.9) consistently top the continent. Southern Africa has the highest sustained Gini readings globally.
insights.northAfricaTitle: North Africa is significantly more equal
insights.northAfricaDesc: Egypt (31.9), Algeria (27.6), and Tunisia (32.8) are the most equal economies in Africa — with Gini scores comparable to many European countries.
insights.sparseDataDesc: Most countries have only 2–4 survey readings over 20 years. Changes between readings may reflect methodology shifts as much as real inequality trends. Interpret with caution.

footer.left: Gini index — income or consumption inequality within countries
